===''Pokémon Horizons: The Series''=== Brassius was first mentioned in ''[[HZ009|Welcome to Paldea!]]'', with [[Alex]] mentioning that he has seen a rare Pokémon, which [[Liko]] theorized could've been the [[Black Rayquaza]]. Brassius debuted in ''[[HZ010|Nemona and Brassius and…]]'', where he was revealed to suffer from artist's block and no longer could see any quality in any of his works. Liko, [[Roy]], and [[Nemona]] stopped by his house for information. Brassius immediately took a liking to Liko upon hearing that she is Alex's daughter, inviting her and her friends to his home. Upon being enraged by Roy's question about Rayquaza, he ended the conversation. Later on, after seeing Liko, Roy, and Nemona's motivation to cheer him up by gathering together dozens of {{p|Sunflora}}, Brassius decided to show them his Rayquaza sculpture, explaining that his encounter with Rayquaza had caused his slump. Seeing Roy's animation, Brassius decided to challenge Roy to a battle. With renewed spirit, Brassius managed to win the battle. Before the group left, Brassius decided to show them one more sculpture, this time of a {{pkmn2|giant}} {{p|Arboliva}} that he saw in the forest where Rayquaza appeared. [[File:Roy and Brassius.png|left|thumb|220px|Brassius and Roy]] In ''[[HZ015|Someone We Can't See! Whosawhatsit?]]'', while the [[Rising Volt Tacklers]] were searching for Liko, Brassius was mentioned by Roy as one of the people he visited to find out where she might have gone. Brassius appeared in a flashback to Liko and Roy's journey in ''[[HZ045|From So Far Away]]''. In ''[[HZ046|Welcome to Naranja Academy!]]'', Brassius was selected as Roy's challenge as part of Tera Training. Liko, Roy, and [[Dot]] arrived in Artazon in ''[[HZ048|Sparkle! The Glow of Flame and Art]]'' in order for Roy to challenge him, but Brassius was busy finishing an sculpture to be featured in the [[Artazon Art Festival]]. After seeing Roy's way of battling and his reaction after losing to Nemona, however, he put the test on indefinite hold, declaring Roy can never master {{Tera}}stallization until he finds out what he's missing. Roy followed his advice and, working with his {{TP|Roy|Fuecoco}}, made a sculpture for the festival that showed to Brassius that he had figured it out. Then, they proceeded to the battle court, where the test was held, and Roy managed to win after Terastallizing Fuecoco. In ''[[HZ055|Showdown! The Paldea Elite Four]]'', Brassius attended the [[Naranja Academy]] as was partnered with Roy as part of the [[Elite Four Tag Battle]]. They battled [[Hassel]], using Fuecoco and Dolliv against {{p|Baxcalibur}} and {{p|Flapple}}, only to eventually lose the match. In ''[[HZ067|Shine on, Terastallization! Liko vs. Roy!]]'', Brassius and Hassel watched Roy's battle against Liko, Brassius praised Roy for his improved battling skills and was pleased when he won the match. {{-}} ====Character==== Brassius is a passionate artist who uses Pokémon as his inspiration for his artwork. He uses the word "avant-garde" when something intrigues or upsets him. As a [[Gym Leader]], he uses battling as a way to teach {{pkmn|Trainer}}s and improve their battling skills. ====Pokémon==== =====On hand===== {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Brassius |pkmn=Dolliv |type1=Grass |type2=Normal |img=Brassius Dolliv.png |epnum=HZ010 |epname=Nemona and Brassius and… |vajp=Arisa Shida |vaen=Alejandra Reynoso |desc={{p|Dolliv}} is Brassius's first known Pokémon. It is often seen with him and has a habit of spraying out olive oil when surprised or happy. It reappeared in ''[[HZ048|Sparkle! The Glow of Flame and Art]]''. In ''[[HZ055|Showdown! The Paldea Elite Four]]'', Brassius used Dolliv in his match against [[Hassel]], where it teamed up with [[Roy's Fuecoco]] to battle {{p|Baxcalibur}} and {{p|Flapple}}, only to be defeated. During the [[Elite Four Tag Battle]], Dolliv proved to be an extremely powerful and versatile Pokémon in battle, serving as support for Fuecoco while battling on equal terms even with the type disadvantage against fully evolved Pokémon. Dolliv's known moves are {{m|Seed Bomb}}, {{m|Helping Hand}}, {{m|Grass Knot}}, and {{m|Energy Ball}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Brassius |pkmn=Sudowoodo |tera=Grass |type1=Rock |img=Brassius Sudowoodo.png |cap=Brassius's Sudowoodo |img2=Brassius Sudowoodo Terastal.png |cap2=Brassius's Terastallized Sudowoodo |epnum=HZ010 |epname=Nemona and Brassius and… |vajp=Tatsuki Kobe |vaen=Bill Rogers |desc={{p|Sudowoodo}} is Brassius's second known Pokémon. It is capable of {{Tera}}stallizing into a {{t|Grass}} type. Brassius used it in his battle against [[Roy]] and his {{TP|Roy|Fuecoco}}. Despite Fuecoco learning {{m|Stomping Tantrum}} during the battle, Sudowoodo was still able to defeat it after Terastallizing. In ''[[HZ048|Sparkle! The Glow of Flame and Art]]'', Sudowoodo was used to battle Roy and his Fuecoco a second time for his [[Tera Training]] test, only to be defeated. Sudowoodo's known moves are {{m|Rock Throw}}, {{m|Substitute}}, {{m|Taunt}}, {{m|Trailblaze}}, and {{m|Stone Edge}}.}} =====Traveling with===== {| width="50%" | {{ActivePoké|||nick=Rotom Phone|Brassius Rotom Phone.png|size=250px|electric|ghost|link=Rotom Phone}} | | |} ====Works of art==== {| class="roundy" style="margin:auto; text-align:center; background:#{{Grass color dark}}; border:5px solid #{{Grass color}}" |- style="background:#eee;" | class="roundytop" style="overflow:hidden;" | [[File:Artazon Festival entrance.png|x120px]] | class="roundytop" style="overflow:hidden;" | [[File:Sunflora at dawn.png|x120px]] |- style="background:#{{grass color light}};" | class="roundybottom" | "{{pcolor|Sunflora|000}} in the eyes of the {{color2|000|Black Rayquaza|black dragon}}" | class="roundybottom" | "{{pcolor|Sunflora|000}} at dawn" |- style="background:#{{grass color light}};"' |- style="background:#eee;" |} ====Voice actors==== {{vatable|color={{grass color}}|bordercolor={{grass color dark}} |ja=中井和哉 ''Kazuya Nakai'' |en=[[J.
